target.title: Guard Skill Workshop update applies against delta drafts
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes a Skill Workshop update-apply footgun where an update proposal that contains a delta/proposal note can be applied as the full replacement `SKILL.md`.

For update proposals, `applySkillProposal()` strips proposal frontmatter and writes the proposal body as the live skill content. That is correct when the proposal body is a full updated skill, but unsafe when the body is an update note such as `# Update Proposal ...` with "proposed changes" instructions. In that case apply can replace the existing skill workflow with a proposal stub.

This PR adds an apply-time guard for update proposals before the live write.

## What changed

- Reject update proposal bodies that look like delta/proposal notes instead of complete skill bodies.
- Reject update proposal bodies that change the top-level skill identity heading.
- Reject suspicious destructive shrinkage where most existing `##` sections disappear and the proposed body is much shorter.
- Add regression coverage for:
  - delta/proposal-note update drafts;
  - destructive section-dropping update drafts;
  - preserving the live skill file and keeping the proposal pending on rejection.

## Duplicate/adjacent work check

I checked current `origin/main` and searched open/closed issues and PRs before opening this PR.

Not duplicates:

- #92733 touches `applySkillProposal()` for openclaw-managed skill roots, but it still writes `stripProposalFrontmatterForSkill(content)` directly and does not validate that update content is a complete skill body.
- #94315 / #94249 cover large-proposal apply timeout behavior.
- #90421 / #90388 cover stale proposal reconciliation when a target skill already exists or disappears.
- #95395 / #94947 cover `action=create` proposals that should have targeted existing skills.

This PR is specifically about update-apply content-shape validation before overwriting an existing `SKILL.md`.

## Validation

Behavior addressed: update proposals that are delta/proposal notes should not overwrite the live skill body.

Real setup tested: isolated source checkout on current `origin/main` with the Skill Workshop service patch applied.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
git diff --check origin/main...HEAD
node <typescript-parser-check for src/skills/workshop/service.ts and src/skills/workshop/service.test.ts>
node <standalone guard reproduction covering delta note, H1 replacement, section drop, and full update acceptance>
<workspace>/.github/scripts/openclaw-heavy-run --backend auto -- pnpm exec vitest run src/skills/workshop/service.test.ts --runInBand
```

Evidence after fix:

- TypeScript parser check passed for the two patched files.
- Standalone guard reproduction rejected:
  - delta/proposal-note update body;
  - H1 identity replacement;
  - section drop / destructive shrink.
- Standalone guard reproduction accepted a complete updated skill body that preserved identity and sections.

Observed result after fix:

The new guard runs before `writeWorkspaceSkill()`, so rejected update proposals do not reach the live `SKILL.md` write path.

What was not tested:

- Full Vitest was not run locally because the guarded heavy-run path deferred to Docker and the matching OpenClaw image was not available locally with image pulls disabled. CI should run the added regression tests.
target.author: anyech
